top_signals.title: The Crypto-Treasury Dream Unravels After a 90% Stock Plunge
top_signals.summary: The business model of launching a public company to buy crypto is falling apart. As a result, those in the queue to do so through blank-check companies are facing pressure from investors against a market backdrop that is fiercely unfriendly.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: July Fed Rate-Hike Wagers Drive Bond Futures Volume Surge
top_signals.summary: The Treasury market saw a record surge in futures trading that underpins bets that the Federal Reserve’s next move will be to boost interest rates.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Decisions taken by the Governing Council of the ECB (in addition to decisions setting interest rates)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Read the 14-point memorandum of understanding between the United States and Iran
top_signals.summary: The 14-point memorandum of understanding between the United States and Iran leaves contentious issues between the countries unresolved.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Klue OAuth breach linked to 'Icarus' Salesforce data theft attacks
top_signals.summary: Market intelligence platform Klue suffered a OAuth breach that enabled the "Icarus" threat actors to steal Salesforce CRM data from multiple organizations in an ongoing extortion campaign. [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: U.S. International Trade in Goods and Services, April 2026
top_signals.summary: The U.S. monthly international trade deficit decreased in April 2026 according to the U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis and the U.S. Census Bureau. The deficit decreased from $56.6 billion in March (revised) to $55.9 billion in April, as exports increased more than imports. The goods deficit decreased $2.4 billion in April to $83.7 billion. The services surplus decreased $1.7 billion in April to $27.8 billion. Full Text
